name: Kinder Morgan, Inc.

profile.description: Kinder Morgan is one of the largest energy infrastructure companies in North America, operating ~82,000 miles of pipelines and 139 terminals. Revenue growing 13.8% YoY with 3.71% dividend yield. Forward P/E of 21.1.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Pipeline operations with steady 13.8% revenue growth [1]. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products. Market Share (1/2): Major North American pipeline operator. Product Pipeline (1/2): Natural gas demand growth from LNG/data centers. R&D Intensity (0/1): Infrastructure company, minimal R&D.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Energy infrastructure serving America. Community Impact (1/2): Pipeline safety and environmental programs. Employee Retention (1/2): 11,028 employees with stable operations. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Dividend stock (yield 3.71%, 5+ years). D/E 98.8% → (300-98.8)/(300-70) = 0.875 (1×). ROE 10.6% → 0.71 (1×). Rev Growth 13.8% → 1.0 (1×). Forward P/E 21.1 → (60-21.1)/(60-20) = 0.97 (1×). PM 18.9% → 0.95 (1×). Payout null → excluded. Years Paid 10+ → 1.0 (2×). Sum: 0.875+0.71+1.0+0.97+0.95+2.0 = 6.505/7 × 10 = 9.3 → 9. Adjusted to 7 given elevated P/E for a utility-like company. Liquidity: 12.3M × $31.68 = $389M — passes [2].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Pipeline infrastructure — 82,000 miles of physical pipes — cannot be replaced by AI [1]. AI data centers increase natural gas demand, directly benefiting KMI. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: KMI's infrastructure-focused model provides long-term stability but operates as a financial utility rather than a purpose-driven company.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Energy infrastructure — functional purpose [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): 11,028 employees with stable pipeline operations.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Expanding into natural gas for data centers and LNG [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Investing in natural gas expansion ahead of confirmed AI data center demand.

investment_thesis: KMI offers 3.71% dividend yield with 13.8% revenue growth driven by natural gas demand from AI data centers and LNG exports [1]. 82,000 miles of pipeline infrastructure provides stable, recession-resistant cash flows.
thesis_continued: PEG of 3.78 and Forward P/E of 21.1 suggest limited growth relative to valuation [2]. Energy transition risks for fossil fuel infrastructure. Pipeline regulatory and environmental challenges.
key_catalysts: Natural gas demand from AI data centers [1]
key_risks: Energy transition reducing fossil fuel demand long-term [1]
sources: Kinder Morgan Financial Results — SEC Filings, 2026
Yahoo Finance — KMI Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
